unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * ***************************************************************************/
/* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */

function.name: set_ipv6_v6only
tcpnodelay

unit.comment: /* The preferred method on Mac OS X (10.2 and later) to prevent SIGPIPEs when sending data to a dead peer (instead of relying on the 4th argument to send being MSG_NOSIGNAL). Possibly also existing and in use on other BSD systems? */

function.name: nosigpipe

unit.comment: /* DragonFlyBSD and Windows use millisecond units */

function.name: tcpkeepalive

unit.comment: /** * Assign the address `ai` to the Curl_sockaddr_ex `dest` and * set the transport used. */

function.name: Curl_sock_assign_addr
socket_open

unit.comment: /* * Create a socket based on info from 'conn' and 'ai'. * * 'addr' should be a pointer to the correct struct to get data back, or NULL. * 'sockfd' must be a pointer to a socket descriptor. * * If the open socket callback is set, used that! * */

function.name: Curl_socket_open
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, const struct Curl_addrinfo *ai, struct Curl_sockaddr_ex *addr, int transport, curl_socket_t *sockfd)

function.name: socket_close
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, int use_callback, curl_socket_t sock)

unit.comment: /* * Close a socket. * * 'conn' can be NULL, beware! */

function.name: Curl_socket_close

unit.comment:
/* When you run a program that uses the Windows Sockets API, you may experience slow performance when you copy data to a TCP server.
https://support.microsoft.com/kb/823764
Work-around: Make the Socket Send Buffer Size Larger Than the Program Send Buffer Size
The problem described in this knowledge-base is applied only to pre-Vista Windows. Following function trying to detect OS version and skips SO_SNDBUF adjustment for Windows Vista and above. */

function.name: Curl_sndbufset
bindlocal
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t sockfd, int af, unsigned int scope)

function.name: verifyconnect
socket_connect_result

unit.comment: /* We have a recv buffer to enhance reads with len < NW_SMALL_READS. * This happens often on TLS connections where the TLS implementation * tries to read the head of a TLS record, determine the length of the * full record and then make a subsequent read for that. * On large reads, we will not fill the buffer to avoid the double copy. */
unit.struct: struct cf_socket_ctx { int transport; struct Curl_sockaddr_ex addr; /* address to connect to */ curl_socket_t sock; /* current attempt socket */ struct bufq recvbuf; /* used when `buffer_recv` is set */ struct ip_quadruple ip; /* The IP quadruple 2x(addr+port) */ struct curltime started_at; /* when socket was created */ struct curltime connected_at; /* when socket connected/got first byte */ struct curltime first_byte_at; /* when first byte was recvd */ int error; /* errno of last failure or 0 */ #ifdef DEBUGBUILD int wblock_percent; /* percent of writes doing EAGAIN */ int wpartial_percent; /* percent of bytes written in send */ int rblock_percent; /* percent of reads doing EAGAIN */ size_t recv_max; /* max enforced read size */ #endif BIT(got_first_byte); /* if first byte was received */ BIT(accepted); /* socket was accepted, not connected */ BIT(sock_connected); /* socket is "connected", e.g. in UDP */ BIT(active); BIT(buffer_recv); };

function.name: cf_socket_ctx_init
nw_in_read
cf_socket_close
cf_socket_destroy
set_local_ip
set_remote_ip
cf_socket_open
do_connect
cf_tcp_connect
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, bool blocking, bool *done)

function.name: cf_socket_get_host
cf_socket_adjust_pollset
cf_socket_data_pending
cf_socket_send
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, const void *buf, size_t len, CURLcode *err)

function.name: cf_socket_recv
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, char *buf, size_t len, CURLcode *err)

function.name: cf_socket_active
cf_socket_cntrl
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, int event, int arg1, void *arg2)

function.name: cf_socket_conn_is_alive
cf_socket_query
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, int query, int *pres1, void *pres2)

unit.decl_stmt: struct Curl_cftype Curl_cft_tcp = { "TCP", CF_TYPE_IP_CONNECT, CURL_LOG_LVL_NONE, cf_socket_destroy, cf_tcp_connect, cf_socket_close, cf_socket_get_host, cf_socket_adjust_pollset, cf_socket_data_pending, cf_socket_send, cf_socket_recv, cf_socket_cntrl, cf_socket_conn_is_alive, Curl_cf_def_conn_keep_alive, cf_socket_query, };

function.name: Curl_cf_tcp_create
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter **pcf, struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, const struct Curl_addrinfo *ai, int transport)

function.name: cf_udp_setup_quic
cf_udp_connect
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, bool blocking, bool *done)

unit.decl_stmt: struct Curl_cftype Curl_cft_udp = { "UDP", CF_TYPE_IP_CONNECT, CURL_LOG_LVL_NONE, cf_socket_destroy, cf_udp_connect, cf_socket_close, cf_socket_get_host, cf_socket_adjust_pollset, cf_socket_data_pending, cf_socket_send, cf_socket_recv, cf_socket_cntrl, cf_socket_conn_is_alive, Curl_cf_def_conn_keep_alive, cf_socket_query, };

function.name: Curl_cf_udp_create
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter **pcf, struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, const struct Curl_addrinfo *ai, int transport)

unit.comment: /* this is the TCP filter which can also handle this case */
unit.decl_stmt: struct Curl_cftype Curl_cft_unix = { "UNIX", CF_TYPE_IP_CONNECT, CURL_LOG_LVL_NONE, cf_socket_destroy, cf_tcp_connect, cf_socket_close, cf_socket_get_host, cf_socket_adjust_pollset, cf_socket_data_pending, cf_socket_send, cf_socket_recv, cf_socket_cntrl, cf_socket_conn_is_alive, Curl_cf_def_conn_keep_alive, cf_socket_query, };

function.name: Curl_cf_unix_create
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter **pcf, struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, const struct Curl_addrinfo *ai, int transport)

function.name: cf_tcp_accept_connect
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, bool blocking, bool *done)

unit.decl_stmt: struct Curl_cftype Curl_cft_tcp_accept = { "TCP-ACCEPT", CF_TYPE_IP_CONNECT, CURL_LOG_LVL_NONE, cf_socket_destroy, cf_tcp_accept_connect, cf_socket_close, cf_socket_get_host, /* TODO: not accurate */ cf_socket_adjust_pollset, cf_socket_data_pending, cf_socket_send, cf_socket_recv, cf_socket_cntrl, cf_socket_conn_is_alive, Curl_cf_def_conn_keep_alive, cf_socket_query, };

function.name: Curl_conn_tcp_listen_set
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, int sockindex, curl_socket_t *s)

function.name: set_accepted_remote_ip
Curl_conn_tcp_accepted_set
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, int sockindex, curl_socket_t *s)

function.name: cf_is_socket
Curl_cf_socket_peek
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data, curl_socket_t *psock, const struct Curl_sockaddr_ex **paddr, struct ip_quadruple *pip)
